title: Composition
summary: Integrate knowledge deduplication with agent error recovery by merging workflows and validating outputs to enhance knowledge base accuracy and resilience.
standard: Combine knowledge deduplication and error recovery processes to create a unified knowledge base with improved accuracy and error handling.
procedure: Run SPARQL queries to extract overlapping entities from both knowledge bases with automated substep verification; assert substep_pass_count = substep_total and error_count < 1; persist results before advancing.

Invoke RDF Schema to merge artifacts, ensuring compatibility and consistency by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Execute SHACL constraints to validate the merged output for structural integrity by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.

Execute a machine learning model to detect candidate errors in the merged data following the defined protocol with boundary checks; assert exit_code < 1 and success_indicator = true; emit completion_event on success.

Assign results using a decision tree to determine error recovery actions using a deterministic decision matrix: evaluate each criterion in priority order; assign first matching class or UNCLASSIFIED; assert confidence_score >= 0.7 for all classified records.
failure_modes: Inconsistent data due to improper merging.
Loss of critical information during deduplication; this occurs when the triggering event is not handled at the correct boundary, causing the condition to surface in a downstream stage.
Increased error rates if recovery steps are misaligned; this occurs when required context is unavailable at execution time, causing the step to proceed with incomplete information.
verification: Check that merged entities maintain unique identifiers; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Ensure SHACL validation passes for all merged data; assert success_count >= expected_count and failure_count < 1.
Verify error recovery actions reduce error rates by 20%; assert output_valid = true and error_count < 1.
